programme.desc: Growing up is hard.

programme.title: Hustle, Sizzle, and Smoke - Steak and Sweet Onion
programme.desc: The competition grows fiercer as Chef Rachelle of Rachelles Creative Dishes squares off against Chef Ohh. With Steak and Sweet Onion as the ingredients, our competitors must dish out sweet and savory meals. Who will secure a chance to continue?

programme.title: Hustle, Sizzle, and Smoke - Salmon and Kale
programme.desc: Get ready for a seafood showdown! Chef Melody of Serenita Homestead grills it up against Chef Don of Carsons BBQ.

programme.title: Hustle, Sizzle, and Smoke - Rib Tips and Apple
programme.desc: Chef Stanzee of Cupcakes Kitchen battles it out against Chef Adrian of DaMunchies ATL; featuring rib tips and apples, the competitors must delicately balance two different flavors to impress the judges with their dishes.

programme.title: Hustle, Sizzle, and Smoke - Catfish and Polenta
programme.desc: Southern staples, catfish and polenta, are on the menu today; Chef Jazurri throws down against Chef Ohh with a trip to the semifinals on the line.

programme.title: Move When The Spirit Says Move: The Legacy of Dorothy Foreman Cotton
programme.desc: Exploring the work of a bold yet unsung civil rights activist and educator and those carrying on her legacy.

programme.title: The First Class
programme.desc: A look at students and educators at Memphis High School reveals the impact of a city reimagining education together.

programme.title: Family Time - Black or White
programme.desc: Tony grows worried when he discovers that Devins college roommate is white; Ebony prepares for her school debate.

programme.title: Family Time - The Aftermath
programme.desc: Now that Devin is out of the house, Lisa suffers from empty nest syndrome and decides to enroll in community college; Melindas new boyfriend offers to buy Tonys business.

programme.title: Family Time - Happy Homecoming
programme.desc: Ebony prepares for homecoming and gets some advice from her mother and aunts about who should be her escort; Melinda helps Tony with his taxes.

programme.title: Family Time - Mancrush
programme.desc: Tony becomes great friends with Cheryls new love, Maxwell; Lisa develops an online shopping addiction.

programme.title: Family Time - The Good Neighbors
programme.desc: As Halloween approaches, Tony grows suspicious of his new neighbors; Ebony feels uncertain about attending her debutante ball, even though it is a family tradition.

programme.title: The Bernie Mac Show - J-O-R-D-A-N Spells Funny
programme.desc: Jordan tries comedy as a way of overcoming stage fright; Vanessa makes a documentary about her home life.

programme.title: The Bernie Mac Show - Make Room for Caddy
programme.desc: Despite Bernies offer to buy him a car, Wandas father cannot get along with him; showing when they all met.

programme.title: The Bernie Mac Show - Saving Sergeant Tompkins
programme.desc: Bernie realizes he may have been too truthful when telling Jordan about his father; he amends the truth to make Jordan think well of his father.

programme.title: The Bernie Mac Show - Its Mac-ademic
programme.desc: Bernie volunteers as a teachers helper to assess the teaching methods used at Bryanas school; Wanda is at wits end trying to reason with Vanessa and Jordan.

programme.title: The Bernie Mac Show - The Getaway
programme.desc: The family goes to San Diego for a relaxing vacation; Bernie, unable to relax, goes to Las Vegas for some gambling and debauchery.

programme.title: The Steve Harvey Show - Detention Peddler
programme.desc: A daunting interview for the position of vice principal derails Steves confidence; Cedric pops the question to Lovita.

programme.title: The Steve Harvey Show - Party of Five
programme.desc: Steve signs up gorgeous twins (Rosey and Renee Tenison) to replace Cedric and Lovita as his roommates.

programme.title: The Steve Harvey Show - Hes Gotta Have It
programme.desc: Steve and Regina clash when they share an office; Cedric loses his sex drive after a computer program predicts that his and Lovitas child will be ugly.

programme.title: The Steve Harvey Show - Little Stevie Blunder
programme.desc: Steve (Steve Harvey) woos a beautiful woman (guest star Kellita Smith) by pretending to be blind; Lovita (Terri J. Vaughn) discovers Cedrics (Cedric The Entertainer) secret locked box.

programme.title: The Steve Harvey Show - Roomies
programme.desc: Regina (Wendy Raquel Robinson) drives Steve (Steve Harvey) buggy when she moves in with him while her apartment is being fumigated.

programme.title: The Steve Harvey Show - My Whole Left Side
programme.desc: Lovita (Terri J. Vaughn) welcomes the opportunity to impress Cedrics (Cedric The Entertainer) beloved Grandma Puddin.

programme.desc: When the girl of his dreams gets kidnapped, a man turns his inability to feel pain into an unexpected advantage. He fights a bunch of thugs to get her back.

programme.title: Criminal Law (1988)
programme.desc: A Boston lawyer (Gary Oldman) gets his rich client (Kevin Bacon) off for murder, then realizes hes still out there killing.

programme.title: The Babysitter (1995)
programme.desc: An alluring teenage sitter (Alicia Silverstone) becomes the object of her suburban clients dreams and obsessions.

programme.title: For a Few Dollars More (1965)
programme.desc: A man with no name (Clint Eastwood) and a man with a mission (Lee Van Cleef) hunt a Mexican bandit (Gian Maria Volonte) for different reasons.

programme.title: The Longest Yard (1974)
programme.desc: A warden (Eddie Albert) forces an ex-football star (Burt Reynolds) to lead fellow inmates in a game against the guards.

programme.title: North Dallas Forty (1979)
programme.desc: The fast life begins to take its toll on two fun-loving but aging football players. Based on Peter Gents novel.

programme.title: Blue Chips (1994)
programme.desc: Corrupt recruiting practices cast a cloud over a college basketball coachs discovery of three amazing players.

programme.title: Hoosiers (1986)
programme.desc: A college basketball coach (Gene Hackman) leaves the Navy in 1951 and becomes coach of an underdog Indiana high-school team.

programme.title: Coach Carter (2005)
programme.desc: A high-school basketball coach (Samuel L. Jackson) turns a losing team around, then faces criticism for pushing the athletes to put grades first.

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Boyd tries to save Acosta from herself as Julie digs deeper into her newfound abilities; Tabitha embarks on a desperate gamble and Victor joins Ethan in a quest for answers.

programme.title: Major League (1989)
programme.desc: Lackluster baseball players (Tom Berenger, Charlie Sheen, Corbin Bernsen) hear their Cleveland teams new owner is counting on them to lose.

programme.title: Bull Durham (1988)
programme.desc: A literary baseball groupie (Susan Sarandon) romances a pitcher (Tim Robbins) and a catcher (Kevin Costner) on a minor-league North Carolina team.

programme.title: Eight Men Out (1988)
programme.desc: Disgruntled Chicago White Sox stand trial for allegedly throwing the 1919 World Series for mobster Arnold Rothstein (Michael Lerner).

programme.title: Rollerball (2002)
programme.desc: A businessman (Jean Reno) wants to see more violence in a dangerous sport where athletes (Chris Klein, LL Cool J) already risk their lives.

programme.title: Rollerball (1975)
programme.desc: A corporate dictator (John Houseman) tries to oust the star player (James Caan) of a brutal 21st-century spectator sport.

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Boyd tries to save Acosta from herself as Julie digs deeper into her newfound abilities; Tabitha embarks on a desperate gamble and Victor joins Ethan in a quest for answers.

programme.title: Max 2: White House Hero (2017)
programme.desc: Max, a military service dog, is assigned to serve on the U.S. presidents Secret Service detail.

programme.title: Unseen (2023)
programme.desc: Sam, a gas station clerk, receives a random call from Emily, a nearly blind woman whos running from her murderous ex in the woods. Using a phone, Sam becomes Emilys only chance for survival as she guides the terrified woman through a video call.

programme.title: The Rhythm Section (2020)
programme.desc: Stephanie Patrick veers down a path of self-destruction after a tragic plane crash kills her family. When Stephanie discovers it wasnt an accident, she soon embarks on a bloody quest for revenge to punish those responsible.

programme.title: Scouts Guide to the Zombie Apocalypse (2015)
programme.sub-title: Scouts Guide to the Zombie Apocalypse (2015)

programme.title: Baywatch (2017)
programme.desc: When a dangerous crime wave hits the beach, Mitch Buchannon leads his elite squad of lifeguards on a mission to prove that you dont have to wear a badge to save the bay.

programme.title: Bandits (2001)
programme.desc: Two bank robbers (Bruce Willis, Billy Bob Thornton) fall for a bored housewife (Cate Blanchett) who helps them commit heists across the country.

programme.title: Flashdance (1983)
programme.desc: A Pittsburgh welder works days, dances in a bar at night, dates her boss and chases the dream of acceptance into a prestigious ballet school.

programme.title: Footloose (1984)
programme.desc: A hip Chicago teen (Kevin Bacon) moves to a Midwestern town where, thanks to a pastor (John Lithgow), dancing is outlawed.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991)
programme.desc: Two cyborgs from the future battle over the life of a teen (Edward Furlong) who will someday lead humanity against the machines.

programme.title: Terminator: Dark Fate (2019)
programme.desc: Seasoned warrior Sarah Connor joins forces with a hybrid cyborg human and the T-800 model to protect a young woman from the Rev-9 -- a newly modified and seemingly indestructible liquid Terminator from the future.

programme.title: Run and Gun (2021)
programme.desc: A former criminal gets blackmailed into one last job to collect a mysterious package. After a deadly double-cross, he soon finds himself wounded and on the run from ruthless assassins who will stop at nothing to get what he has.

programme.title: The Corrupted (2019)
programme.desc: Released from prison, a talented boxer wants nothing more than to reconnect with his young son and live a quiet life. But when his brother gets on the wrong side of a shady developer, he must risk everything to save his sibling before its too late.

programme.title: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider (2001)
programme.desc: A beautiful globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) battles otherworldly creatures for possession of a powerful artifact.

programme.title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.sub-title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.desc: A globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) must find Pandoras box before a maniacal scientist (Ciarán Hinds) can harness its power for world domination.

programme.title: Vindicta (2023)
programme.desc: When a city is terrorized by a sadistic serial killer, a seasoned detective and a newly recruited paramedic are forced into a deadly game of vengeance, only to discover the key to stopping the bloodshed lies in unlocking the truth of their own pasts.

programme.title: Alone in Berlin (2016)
programme.desc: A German couple lose their son in WWII, prompting them to become secretly anti-Nazi.

programme.title: The Survivalist (2021)
programme.desc: A year and a half after the fall of civilization due to a viral outbreak, a former FBI agent is forced to protect a young woman immune to the disease from a dangerous gang leader hunting her.

programme.title: Abandon (2002)
programme.desc: A detective (Benjamin Bratt) discovers new facts regarding the disappearance of a collegians (Katie Holmes) boyfriend (Charlie Hunnam) two years earlier.

programme.title: Urban Cowboy (1980)
programme.desc: A Texas farmer (John Travolta) moves to the city, immerses himself in honky-tonk and falls for a sexy cowgirl (Debra Winger).

programme.title: Saving Private Ryan (1998)
programme.desc: A World War II captain (Tom Hanks) and his squad (Edward Burns, Tom Sizemore) risk all to locate and send home a soldier whose three brothers died in combat.

programme.title: No Code of Conduct (1998)
programme.desc: Two policemen (Mark Dacascos, Charlie Sheen) learn that city leaders and law enforcement support heroin trafficking across the Mexican border.

programme.title: Face/Off (1997)
programme.desc: An FBI agent (John Travolta) and his nemesis (Nicolas Cage) trade physical identities.

programme.title: Reindeer Games (2000)
programme.desc: An ex-convict (Ben Affleck) wants to start a new life with his girlfriend (Charlize Theron), but her brother (Gary Sinise) involves him in a scheme to rob a casino.

programme.title: Saving Private Ryan (1998)
programme.desc: A World War II captain (Tom Hanks) and his squad (Edward Burns, Tom Sizemore) risk all to locate and send home a soldier whose three brothers died in combat.

programme.title: No Country for Old Men (2007)
programme.desc: An aging lawman (Tommy Lee Jones) reflects on his past and laments a changing world while trying to find and protect a hunter who took the cash from a drug deal gone bad.

programme.title: Shell (2024)
programme.desc: Desperate to reclaim her career, once-beloved actress Samantha Lake gets drawn into the glamorous world of wellness mogul Zoe Shannon, only to uncover a monstrous truth beneath the seemingly flawless surface.

programme.title: V for Vengeance (2022)
programme.desc: After learning that their younger sister escaped an earlier kidnapping attempt that also killed their parents, two estranged sisters must join forces to rescue her from a group of bloodthirsty vampires.

programme.title: Reindeer Games (2000)
programme.desc: An ex-convict (Ben Affleck) wants to start a new life with his girlfriend (Charlize Theron), but her brother (Gary Sinise) involves him in a scheme to rob a casino.

programme.title: Cypher (2002)
programme.desc: A man (Jeremy Northam) soon becomes confused about his own identity after he becomes a corporate spy for each of two rival firms.

programme.title: Most Wanted (2020)
programme.desc: An investigative reporter fights to expose the twisted truth behind a heroin bust orchestrated by dirty cops to frame an innocent man -- sentencing him to life in a Thai prison.

programme.title: Mob Town (2019)
programme.desc: The FBI and a local cop track various members of organized crime families as they gather for a huge meeting in upstate New York in 1957.

programme.title: Paycheck (2003)
programme.desc: After learning that his memory has been erased, a technical wizard (Ben Affleck) goes on the run to piece together clues from his past.

programme.title: The Hunt for Red October (1990)
programme.desc: Moscow, Washington and a CIA analyst (Alec Baldwin) track a renegade Soviet captain (Sean Connery) and his new submarine.

programme.title: Mindhunters (2004)
programme.desc: A serial killer targets a group of FBI trainees (Jonny Lee Miller, Kathryn Morris) and a Philadelphia policeman (LL Cool J) on a deserted island.

programme.title: Flags of Our Fathers (2006)
programme.desc: Some of the servicemen who raised the U.S. flag on Mount Suribachi during the Battle of Iwo Jima at the end of World War II live to hear of their status as heroes.

programme.title: Most Wanted (2020)
programme.desc: An investigative reporter fights to expose the twisted truth behind a heroin bust orchestrated by dirty cops to frame an innocent man -- sentencing him to life in a Thai prison.

programme.title: Ida Red (2021)
programme.desc: Crime boss Ida "Red" Walker turns to her son, Wyatt, to pull off one last heist to get out of prison. However, with the FBI closing in, Wyatt must soon choose between family and freedom.

programme.title: Hamburger Hill (1987)
programme.desc: An account of the 101st Airborne Divisions 10-day struggle to secure a strategic mountainside in South Vietnam.

programme.title: Before We Vanish (2017)
programme.desc: Three aliens travel to Earth on a mission in preparation for a mass invasion. Having taken possession of human bodies, the visitors rob their hosts of the very essence of their being, leaving psychological and spiritual devastation in their wake.

programme.title: Fist of Fury (1972)
programme.desc: In turn-of-the-century China, a martial-arts student declares war against the rival fighters who murdered his mentor.

programme.title: Bruce Lee: The Legend (1984)
programme.desc: The martial artist is seen in clips and recalled by Steve McQueen, James Coburn and others who knew him.

programme.title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.sub-title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.desc: A globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) must find Pandoras box before a maniacal scientist (Ciarán Hinds) can harness its power for world domination.

programme.title: Breakdown (1997)
programme.desc: A mans (Kurt Russell) wife (Kathleen Quinlan) disappears in the desert Southwest after accepting a truckers (J.T. Walsh) help with car trouble.

programme.title: Uncommon Valor (1983)
programme.desc: A colonel with a sponsor leads five Marine veterans into Laos to find his son and others missing in action.

programme.title: Flight of the Intruder (1991)
programme.desc: Two Navy pilots (Willem Dafoe, Brad Johnson) hatch a rogue mission to bomb Hanoi with a special low-altitude plane.

programme.title: Gasoline Alley (2022)
programme.desc: Set in the underbelly of Los Angeles, a man is drawn into a high-profile triple murder. The two police detectives on his tail soon join him in attempting to uncover an explosive conspiracy.

programme.title: The Contractor (2022)
programme.desc: When his new assignment goes awry, an elite soldier finds himself hunted and on the run, caught in a dangerous conspiracy and fighting to stay alive long enough to get home and uncover the true motives of those who betrayed him.

programme.title: The Peacemaker (1997)
programme.desc: A scientist (Nicole Kidman) and an Army Special Forces colonel (George Clooney) travel the globe in pursuit of stolen nuclear weapons.

programme.title: Cold Mountain (2003)
programme.desc: During the Civil War, a wounded Confederate soldier (Jude Law) abandons his duties to make his way home to his sweetheart (Nicole Kidman).

programme.title: The Quiet American (2002)
programme.desc: A veteran English journalist (Michael Caine) and a young man (Brendan Fraser) vie for the affection of a Vietnamese woman (Do Thi Hai Yen) in the 1950s.

programme.title: Backcountry (2014)
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bears territory.

programme.title: The Peacemaker (1997)
programme.desc: A scientist (Nicole Kidman) and an Army Special Forces colonel (George Clooney) travel the globe in pursuit of stolen nuclear weapons.

programme.title: Nothing Compares (2022)
programme.desc: Following the career of singer Sinead OConnor through her rise to fame and how her iconoclastic personality led to her exile from the pop mainstream.

programme.title: Spotlights: A Showtime Short Film Series
programme.desc: A collection of short films showcasing different themes.

programme.title: Election (1999)
programme.desc: Tracy faces no competition in her high school election, but the civics teacher intervenes by encouraging the varsity football player to run for president, resulting in chaos.

programme.title: Parallel (2024)
programme.desc: Following the journey of Vanessa, a grief-stricken woman who mysteriously finds herself navigating between parallel spaces.

programme.title: The Back-up Plan (2010)
programme.desc: A single woman (Jennifer Lopez) meets the man (Alex OLoughlin) of her dreams on the same day she becomes pregnant through artificial insemination.

programme.title: The Aviator (2004)
programme.desc: During the 1930s and 40s, wealthy industrialist Howard Hughes (Leonardo DiCaprio) gains fame as a movie producer, airplane designer and pilot.

programme.title: Kate and Leopold (2001)
programme.desc: A Manhattan woman (Meg Ryan) falls in love with the 19th-century Duke of Albany (Hugh Jackman) who has stepped through a time portal.

programme.title: Losing Isaiah (1995)
programme.desc: An ex-crack addict (Halle Berry) fights for custody of her son adopted by a social worker (Jessica Lange) and her husband (David Strathairn).

programme.title: Terms of Endearment (1983)
programme.desc: A strong-willed woman (Shirley MacLaine) and her equally independent daughter (Debra Winger) keep the lines of communication open as each deals with the curveballs life throws at them.

programme.title: The Evening Star (1996)
programme.desc: A spunky woman (Shirley MacLaine) copes with her troubled adult grandchildren, feuds with an enemy and has a fling with a younger man (Bill Paxton).

programme.title: Fatal Attraction (1987)
programme.desc: A New York lawyer (Michael Douglas) with a wife (Anne Archer) and daughter sleeps with a client (Glenn Close) whose lust turns to hate.

programme.title: Scream 4 (2011)
programme.desc: The Ghostface Killer returns with a vengeance when self-help author Sidney Prescott arrives in Woodsboro on the last stop of her book tour.

programme.title: Miles to Go Before I Sleep
programme.desc: The relationships between musicians and their road crews and the importance of the people who work to put shows together.

programme.title: Losing Isaiah (1995)
programme.desc: An ex-crack addict (Halle Berry) fights for custody of her son adopted by a social worker (Jessica Lange) and her husband (David Strathairn).

programme.title: A Mighty Heart (2007)
programme.desc: Mariane Pearl (Angelina Jolie) begins a terrifying search for her husband, journalist Daniel Pearl (Dan Futterman), after he is kidnapped by terrorists in 2002 Pakistan.

programme.title: Alone in Berlin (2016)
programme.desc: A German couple lose their son in WWII, prompting them to become secretly anti-Nazi.

programme.title: Heartburn (1986)
programme.desc: Rachel Samstat, a New York food critic, beds Mark Forman, a Washington, D.C., newspaper columnist. The two fall in love, and Rachel relinquishes her job and relocates to be with a man who views himself as unfit for marriage.

programme.title: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider (2001)
programme.desc: A beautiful globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) battles otherworldly creatures for possession of a powerful artifact.

programme.title: Top Gun: Maverick (2022)
programme.desc: Pete "Maverick" Mitchell keeps pushing the envelope after years of service as one of the Navys top aviators. He must soon confront the past while training a new squad of graduates for a dangerous mission that demands the ultimate sacrifice.

programme.title: Guns Up (2025)
programme.desc: After a job goes horribly wrong, a man who moonlights as a mob henchman has one night to get his family out of the city while keeping his dark secret hidden.

programme.title: Shakespeare in Love (1998)
programme.desc: Young William Shakespeare (Joseph Fiennes) falls for Viola (Gwyneth Paltrow), reawakening his creativity, but she is betrothed to Wessex.

programme.title: A Bad Moms Christmas (2017)
programme.desc: As if creating the perfect Christmas for their families isnt hard enough, under-appreciated and overburdened moms Amy, Kiki and Carla will have to do it while hosting and entertaining their own respective mothers during the holidays.

programme.title: The Guilt Trip (2012)
programme.desc: Pressured into taking his overbearing mother along for the ride, a man embarks on the road trip of a lifetime.

programme.title: Yes, Madam (1985)
programme.desc: A relentless inspector (Michelle Yeoh) and a Scotland Yard detective (Cynthia Rothrock) investigate murder linked to microfilm.

programme.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley (1999)
programme.desc: A young man (Matt Damon) travels to 1950s Italy and begins to assume the identity of a playboy (Jude Law) he was hired to bring back to the United States.

programme.title: Cursed (2005)
programme.desc: Siblings (Christina Ricci, Jesse Eisenberg) hunt for the werewolf that attacked them and transformed them into lycanthropes.

programme.title: Alone in Berlin (2016)
programme.desc: A German couple lose their son in WWII, prompting them to become secretly anti-Nazi.

programme.title: The Quiet American (2002)
programme.desc: A veteran English journalist (Michael Caine) and a young man (Brendan Fraser) vie for the affection of a Vietnamese woman (Do Thi Hai Yen) in the 1950s.

programme.title: Outside Providence (1999)
programme.desc: After a young man (Shawn Hatosy) crashes into a police cruiser, his father sends him to boarding school where he struggles to be accepted.

programme.title: The Wedding Banquet (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to stay in the country, a gay man proposes a green card marriage to a female friend in exchange for paying for her IVF treatment. However, things get complicated when his grandmother surprises them with an extravagant Korean wedding banquet.

programme.title: The Friend (2024)
programme.desc: Iris finds her comfortable New York City life thrown into disarray after her closest friend and mentor bequeaths her a Great Dane named Apollo. Yet as Iris finds herself unexpectedly bonding with Apollo, she begins to come to terms with her past.

programme.title: I Used to Go Here (2020)
programme.desc: A successful author finds herself deeply enmeshed in the lives of a group of college students after being asked to speak at her alma mater.

programme.title: Last Weekend (2014)
programme.desc: Petty squabbling marks the Memorial Day reunion of an affluent matriarch (Patricia Clarkson) and her dysfunctional family (Chris Mulkey, Zachary Booth).

programme.title: Failure to Launch (2006)
programme.desc: The parents of a young man (Matthew McConaughey) who still lives at home hire a beautiful woman (Sarah Jessica Parker) to entice him to finally leave the nest.

programme.title: Bridget Joness Diary (2001)
programme.desc: An outrageous British woman (Renee Zellweger) decides to take control of her life, but instead falls for two very different men (Colin Firth, Hugh Grant).
